But he's in dentistry, now I care, you know. But man, it's a daily deal. And by the way, the more you do, the more visible you are, the more successful you are, the more this you're going to face. The only other answer is of course to outrace them. You know, I mean there's some parts of what we do that sort of gets locked in place and stays there, right. But a lot of what we do is pretty fluid. So then it's that part of the business is Ray Crox's answer, we can invent faster, they can copy. So by the time they're doing what you were doing, you were doing something else, right. There is, it raises sort of a fun point that I'll mention very quickly. In some niches, copycats copy only what they can see, right. And they're too lazy to go look at everything so they only look at the most visible. So often over the years I've had five or six or seven instances with clients where we have for a short period of time, peed away money running really bad ads, right. Knowing that the moron knockoff artist who was stealing all our stuff would copy all those ads and lose his hind end. And we could afford it, he couldn't. And then he'd go away, you know. And we've done it on more than one occasion. And sometimes you do stuff like that, it's like discouragement to entry, you know. On a local basis, Mike Storms is in a crystical business years ago, he's in Meta-Louise, Anna. And he says there's, he's already got like 14 competitors all within drive-in, he says these guys are coming over and I know they're open on two schools right down the road from me. And you know, you got any bright ideas. I said, yeah, I says why don't you rent like five of them billboards all the way down the street, you know, between your school and theirs for three months, put your face up on them and Mike Storms karate. And he says, we're gonna get any students from that? I said, I'm gonna get a damn student. I said, but it's really discouraging to the guy driving down the road thinking about opening up that little school over there when all he sees is your face all the way down the road. Ruh, ruh, ruh, ruh, ruh, ruh, ruh, ruh, ruh. Maybe he'll think twice about this, right. Guy went away, never opened the school. Okay, let's talk about how to get paid. I am on page 109. Some people get paid per hour. Okay, I would think that I'm pretty convinced that's like the normal coaching model that is being taught by the normal coaching institutions who are kicking life coaches and business coaches out at a rapid rate. And so kicking out coaches, not mentioning to them that you can't make any money in this business unless you have a way to get somebody to coach. So that's like problem number one. And then they're teaching them all the hourly model. For the most part, as I said at the very beginning of the seminar, it's, no matter what you charge, you'll ultimately be unhappy because when you trade hours for dollars, you only have so many hours to trade and you can only get so much an hour when it doesn't matter what the value of the advice is that you're dispensing. The person is still watching a clock. They can see 45 minutes have gone by and now it's over and they're only good at, you know, at some point in time, value, no value, it's just too big of a check to write for the hour. Some people do term programs, meaning, and usually they're two end outcome. Very common in the get rich in real estate industry, for example, where there's a coaching program in the last 12 weeks and it's designed to get the person through and to two end through their first transaction. And that's sort of the promise. Perry Marshall, the Google AdWords guy, has a term coaching program. It's X number of things over X number of weeks or months. I forget, it's a set fee and it's done. Some people figure out a way to do coaching per project. Mostly that's consulting, it's not coaching, but some people have coaching groups that are about a project. There's a variation of it, which I'll talk to you about under how to get more money. But some people are doing per project coaching. Some people are doing the retainer stuff, where they're paying by the month or they're paying by the year, essentially for access. And a lot of people screw that up by giving too much access, unrestricted access, uncontrolled access, like coaching program includes, you can email me and get questions answered. Well, 80% won't email you much, but the 20% who do could kill you. And you will always wind up out of every group with a few pen pals who want to write to you eight, 10, 12, 20 times a day. So this unrestricted access thing is generally not a good idea. If you do terms, the best thing is the automatically renewing term.
